name: Patient Health Questionnaire (PHQ-9)
disclaimerBefore: This questionnaire is for educational purposes only and is NOT a clinical diagnosis. Results are for personal reflection. If you have concerns or severe symptoms, please consult a healthcare professional. Your answers are stored only in your browser.
disclaimerAfter: This test is NOT a clinical diagnosis and does NOT replace evaluation by a professional. If you have severe or persistent symptoms, please consult your doctor or psychologist. Based on Kroenke, Spitzer & Williams (2001).

landing.hook: Have you been feeling down or hopeless lately?
landing.description: The PHQ-9 is the world's leading questionnaire for detecting depressive symptoms. It assesses 9 key symptoms of major depressive episode according to DSM-5 criteria, over the past two weeks.
landing.whatItMeasures: It measures 9 depression symptoms: anhedonia (lack of interest or pleasure), depressed mood, sleep disturbances, fatigue, appetite changes, feelings of worthlessness, difficulty concentrating, psychomotor changes, and thoughts of death.
landing.whoIsItFor: Adults (18+) experiencing symptoms of depression or persistent sadness. Also useful for monitoring symptoms in people already receiving treatment.
howItWorks: Answer 9 questions about the past 2 weeks
Get your depression level with detailed clinical interpretation
Share the result with your doctor or psychologist if needed

faq.q: Is this a diagnosis of depression?
faq.a: No. The PHQ-9 is a screening tool, not a diagnosis. Only a qualified mental health professional can diagnose a depressive disorder. This test helps you identify whether your symptoms warrant professional attention.
Yes. All answers are processed in your browser and never sent to any server. We do not store any personal data.

faq.q: What should I do if I score high?
faq.a: We recommend consulting your primary care doctor or a psychologist. If you feel you cannot wait, you can call the 988 Suicide & Crisis Lifeline, available 24 hours.

faq.q: Can I take this test if I am already in treatment?
faq.a: Yes. The PHQ-9 is frequently used in clinical practice to monitor treatment progress. You can share the result with your therapist.
Between 3 and 5 minutes. There are 9 questions with 4 response options each.

questions.text: How often have you had little interest or pleasure in doing things?
How often have you been feeling down, depressed, or hopeless?
How often have you had trouble falling or staying asleep, or sleeping too much?
How often have you been feeling tired or having little energy?
How often have you had poor appetite or been overeating?
How often have you been feeling bad about yourself — or that you are a failure or have let yourself or your family down?
How often have you had trouble concentrating on things, such as reading or watching television?
How often have you been moving or speaking so slowly that other people could have noticed? Or the opposite — being so fidgety or restless that you have been moving around much more than usual?
How often have you had thoughts that you would be better off dead, or thoughts of hurting yourself in some way?

minimal.title: Minimal Depression
minimal.body: Your symptoms are minimal. It is normal to feel low occasionally.
minimal.recommendation: Keep up healthy habits: regular sleep, physical activity, and social connections.

mild.title: Mild Depression
mild.body: You are experiencing mild symptoms that may occasionally affect your daily life.
mild.recommendation: Consider self-care strategies and, if symptoms persist beyond 2 weeks, speak with your doctor.

moderate.title: Moderate Depression
moderate.body: Your symptoms are moderate and are likely affecting your wellbeing in several areas of life.
moderate.recommendation: It is RECOMMENDED that you consult a psychologist or doctor. Early treatment makes a significant difference.

moderately_severe.title: Moderately Severe Depression
moderately_severe.body: Your symptoms are significant and are likely interfering with your work, relationships, and wellbeing.
moderately_severe.recommendation: It is IMPORTANT that you contact a mental health professional as soon as possible.

severe.title: Severe Depression
severe.body: Your symptoms indicate severe depression that requires urgent professional attention.
severe.recommendation: Contact your doctor or psychologist TODAY. If you do not have one, you can call a crisis line for guidance.

crisis.title: You need support right now
crisis.body: You indicated thoughts of harming yourself. That matters deeply. You do not have to face this alone. There are trained people available to listen to you right now.
